mnemonics: G.O.A.T. Gradient: Gradient Of All Terms. Remember to differentiate each term in the MSE sum with respect to the parameter.
The Gradient Points Steepest Up: The gradient vector points in the direction of the steepest increase of the MSE. To minimize, we move opposite to it (gradient descent).
keyPoints: The MSE is a scalar cost function measuring average squared error.
Its gradient is a vector of partial derivatives, one for each model parameter.
Setting the gradient to zero and solving gives the analytical solution for the optimal parameters (normal equations).
In practice, the gradient is used iteratively in algorithms like Gradient Descent to find the minimum.
commonMistakes: Forgetting the factor of 2/N (or 2) that comes from differentiating the squared term.
Mishandling matrix/vector dimensions when calculating the gradient for multivariate problems.
Confusing the gradient (points up) with the descent direction (points down).
